quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Cellular respiration (overall)"?

options.text: Small intracellular molecules (cAMP, Ca²⁺, IP₃) that relay and amplify signals from the receptor to target proteins
C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂ → 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + ~30-32 ATP; the complete aerobic oxidation of glucose
The pathway by which a chemical element (C, N, P, H₂O) moves through the biotic and abiotic components of an ecosystem
Speciation driven by geographic isolation that prevents gene flow between populations, allowing independent evolution

quiz_questions.explanation: "Cellular respiration (overall)" is defined as: C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂ → 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + ~30-32 ATP; the complete aerobic oxidation of glucose. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "ATP yield per glucose"?

options.text: Both alleles are fully expressed in the heterozygote (e.g., Iᴬ Iᴮ blood type produces both A and B antigens)
A group consisting of a common ancestor and ALL of its descendants; the fundamental unit in modern classification
~30-32 ATP total: 2 from glycolysis, 2 from Krebs cycle, ~26-28 from oxidative phosphorylation
The proportion of a specific allele in a population: p = frequency of dominant allele, q = frequency of recessive allele; p + q = 1

quiz_questions.explanation: "ATP yield per glucose" is defined as: ~30-32 ATP total: 2 from glycolysis, 2 from Krebs cycle, ~26-28 from oxidative phosphorylation. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Aerobic vs. anaerobic"?

options.text: The main replication enzyme in E. coli; synthesizes DNA 5'→3' by adding nucleotides complementary to the template strand
Multiple genes contribute to a single trait, producing continuous variation (e.g., skin color, height, intelligence)
Aerobic respiration requires O₂ and yields ~30-32 ATP; anaerobic fermentation produces only 2 ATP per glucose
Failure of chromosomes to separate properly during meiosis, resulting in gametes with abnormal chromosome numbers (aneuploidy)

quiz_questions.explanation: "Aerobic vs. anaerobic" is defined as: Aerobic respiration requires O₂ and yields ~30-32 ATP; anaerobic fermentation produces only 2 ATP per glucose. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Metabolic rate"?

options.text: The percentage of offspring showing recombinant phenotypes; used to calculate the distance between linked genes
The rate of energy expenditure by an organism; basal metabolic rate (BMR) is the minimum rate at rest
A gene-editing system using guide RNA to direct the Cas9 nuclease to cut DNA at a precise location, enabling precise gene knockout, correction, or insertion
The monomer of nucleic acids: a 5-carbon sugar (deoxyribose or ribose), a phosphate group, and a nitrogenous base

quiz_questions.explanation: "Metabolic rate" is defined as: The rate of energy expenditure by an organism; basal metabolic rate (BMR) is the minimum rate at rest. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Redox reactions"?

options.text: Oxidation (loss of electrons/hydrogen) and reduction (gain of electrons/hydrogen); glucose oxidation transfers electrons to O₂ via NAD⁺ and FAD
A cluster of prokaryotic genes under control of a single promoter and operator, transcribed as one polycistronic mRNA (e.g., lac operon, trp operon)
A series of molecular events by which a signal on a cell's surface is converted to a specific cellular response
O₂; accepts electrons at the end of the ETC and combines with H⁺ to form water; without O₂, the ETC stops

quiz_questions.explanation: "Redox reactions" is defined as: Oxidation (loss of electrons/hydrogen) and reduction (gain of electrons/hydrogen); glucose oxidation transfers electrons to O₂ via NAD⁺ and FAD. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂ → 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + ~30-32 ATP; the complete aerobic oxidation of glucose"?

options.text: Habitat destruction
Cellular respiration (overall)
Reproductive isolation
Adaptive radiation

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Cellular respiration (overall)".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "~30-32 ATP total: 2 from glycolysis, 2 from Krebs cycle, ~26-28 from oxidative phosphorylation"?

options.text: ATP yield per glucose
Nonsense mutation
Hardy-Weinberg conditions
Recombinant DNA

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "ATP yield per glucose".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Aerobic respiration requires O₂ and yields ~30-32 ATP; anaerobic fermentation produces only 2 ATP per glucose"?

options.text: Germline vs. somatic mutation
Hardy-Weinberg conditions
Proton-motive force
Aerobic vs. anaerobic

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Aerobic vs. anaerobic".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "The rate of energy expenditure by an organism; basal metabolic rate (BMR) is the minimum rate at rest"?

options.text: Final electron acceptor
Metabolic rate
Nucleus
Species richness

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Metabolic rate".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Oxidation (loss of electrons/hydrogen) and reduction (gain of electrons/hydrogen); glucose oxidation transfers electrons to O₂ via NAD⁺ and FAD"?

options.text: Glycolysis
DNA polymerase III
Redox reactions
Semiconservative replication

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Redox reactions".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about Cellular Respiration is accurate?

options.text: Cellular Respiration (overview) is the complete oxidation of glucose through glycolysis, pyruvate oxidation, the Krebs cycle, and oxidative phosphorylation, yielding approximately 30-32 ATP per glucose molecule.
ATP hydrolysis (ΔG = -30.5 kJ/mol) couples to endergonic reactions to drive cellular work.

Non-Mendelian Inheritance includes patterns that do not follow simple dominant-recessive rules: incomplete dominance (blending phenotypes, e.g., red × white = pink in snapdragons), codominance (both alleles fully expressed, e.g., AB blood type),...
Enzyme specificity arises from the precise 3D shape of the active site, which is determined by the amino acid sequence.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Cellular Respiration as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which statement correctly describes an aspect of Cellular Respiration?

options.text: Conservation Biology integrates ecology, genetics, and evolutionary biology to protect and restore biological diversity.
The overall equation is C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂ → 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + ~30-32 ATP.
Adaptive radiation (rapid diversification from a single ancestor into multiple ecological niches) often follows mass extinctions or colonization of new environments.
Water is split (photolysis) at PSII, releasing O₂.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Cellular Respiration as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which is true specifically about Cellular Respiration?

options.text: Genomic technologies (DNA sequencing, gene editing with CRISPR-Cas9, and bioinformatics) have revolutionized biology, medicine, agriculture, and forensics.
Cellular respiration is regulated at multiple control points to match ATP production to cellular demand, with glucose, fatty acids, and amino acids all serving as fuel sources.
Nucleic Acids (DNA and RNA) store and transmit genetic information through sequences of nucleotide monomers.
Recombination frequency between linked genes (calculated from testcross offspring) indicates the distance between genes on a chromosome, measured in centimorgans (cM) or map units.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Cellular Respiration as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best explains a key idea of Cellular Respiration?

options.text: Cellular Respiration (overview) is the complete oxidation of glucose through glycolysis, pyruvate oxidation, the Krebs cycle, and oxidative phosphorylation, yielding approximately 30-32 ATP per glucose molecule.
Energy and Metabolism describes how cells manage energy through coupled reactions and metabolic pathways.
DNA Replication and Repair describes the semiconservative process by which DNA is copied before cell division.
The central dogma of molecular biology—DNA → RNA → Protein—describes the flow of genetic information in cells.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Cellular Respiration as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which accurately reflects the content on Cellular Respiration?

options.text: Non-Mendelian Inheritance includes patterns that do not follow simple dominant-recessive rules: incomplete dominance (blending phenotypes, e.g., red × white = pink in snapdragons), codominance (both alleles fully expressed, e.g., AB blood type),...
The overall equation is C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂ → 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + ~30-32 ATP.
Populations are characterized by density, age structure, survivorship curves (Type I, II, III), and life history strategies (r-selected vs.

Island biogeography theory (MacArthur and Wilson) predicts that species richness on an island reflects a balance between immigration and extinction rates, influenced by island size and distance from the mainland.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Cellular Respiration as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following is a real-world application of Cellular Respiration?

options.text: Antibiotic targeting of bacterial ribosomes: tetracycline blocks the A-site, chloramphenicol inhibits peptidyl transferase—both exploit differences between prokaryotic and eukaryotic ribosomes

Epinephrine (adrenaline) signaling cascade: one epinephrine molecule activates many G-proteins, each activating adenylyl cyclase producing millions of cAMP molecules, amplifying the fight-or-flight response
Wildlife management using logistic growth models to calculate maximum sustainable yield for harvested populations (fisheries, deer management)
Exercise physiology: transitioning from aerobic to anaerobic respiration during intense activity when O₂ delivery cannot meet demand

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Cellular Respiration.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which example demonstrates the practical relevance of Cellular Respiration?

options.text: Caloric content of foods measured by the energy released during complete oxidation: carbohydrates ~4 kcal/g, lipids ~9 kcal/g, proteins ~4 kcal/g
Galápagos finch adaptive radiation: a single ancestor species diversifying into 14 species with specialized beak shapes for different food sources within ~1-2 million years
α-amanitin (death cap mushroom toxin) inhibiting RNA polymerase II, blocking transcription and causing fatal liver failure within days
Crop yield optimization through understanding light reaction efficiency and the effects of light intensity, wavelength, and duration on photosynthetic rates

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Cellular Respiration.

quiz_questions.question_text: How is Cellular Respiration applied in real-world contexts?

options.text: Capillary action in plant xylem vessels transporting water against gravity from roots to leaves through cohesion-adhesion-tension
Antibiotic targeting of prokaryotic-specific structures (70S ribosomes, cell wall synthesis) that differ from eukaryotic cells, enabling selective bacterial killing
Biofuel production engineering algae with enhanced Calvin cycle efficiency to convert CO₂ into lipids for biodiesel
Metabolic syndrome and diabetes: dysregulation of glucose metabolism affecting cellular respiration, insulin signaling, and energy storage pathways

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Cellular Respiration.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which scenario illustrates the principles of Cellular Respiration?

options.text: Exercise physiology: transitioning from aerobic to anaerobic respiration during intense activity when O₂ delivery cannot meet demand
Madagascar's unique biodiversity (90% of species found nowhere else) explained by 88 million years of isolation after separating from Africa and India
Antibiotic targeting of bacterial ribosomes: tetracycline blocks the A-site, chloramphenicol inhibits peptidyl transferase—both exploit differences between prokaryotic and eukaryotic ribosomes
Human population growth following a roughly exponential pattern from 1 billion (1800) to 8 billion (2022), with demographic transition slowing growth in developed nations

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Cellular Respiration.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best shows Cellular Respiration in practice?

options.text: CRISPR-Cas9 clinical trials treating sickle cell disease by editing the BCL11A gene in patient stem cells to reactivate fetal hemoglobin production
Antibiotic targeting of prokaryotic-specific structures (70S ribosomes, cell wall synthesis) that differ from eukaryotic cells, enabling selective bacterial killing
HPV (human papillomavirus) causing cervical cancer by producing proteins that inactivate p53 and Rb tumor suppressors, disabling cell cycle checkpoints
Caloric content of foods measured by the energy released during complete oxidation: carbohydrates ~4 kcal/g, lipids ~9 kcal/g, proteins ~4 kcal/g

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Cellular Respiration.
